sentences.grammarNote: 실 (sil) means 'room'. 1인실 (il insil) = single room, 2인실 (i insil) = double room. The Sino-Korean number prefix + 인 (person) + 실 (room) pattern applies throughout Korean accommodation vocabulary.

sentences.base_language: What floor is the room on?
sentences.grammarNote: 층 (cheung) = floor/story. Ground floor is 1층 (il cheung). 몇 (myeot) = 'how many / what number', used before counters: 몇 층 (what floor), 몇 시 (what time), 몇 명 (how many people).

sentences.base_language: I'll check out early tomorrow morning.

sentences.grammarNote: 잘 자요 (jal jayo) = 'good night' (literally 'sleep well'). More intimate: 잘 자 (jal ja). The formal 안녕히 주무세요 (annyeonghi jumuseyo) uses an entirely different, honorific verb for sleeping — 주무시다 — reserved for elders and superiors.
